added_assumptions.label: ass:arm-tangent-nondegeneracy
added_assumptions.statement: inf_{b_0,b_1 in R} int (y^2 - b_0 - b_1 y)^2 d nu_a(y) > 0 for each a in {0,1}.

added_assumptions.label: ass:interior-band
added_assumptions.statement: underline_m <= m_a <= overline_m for each a in {0,1}.

added_assumptions.label: ass:tangent-band
added_assumptions.statement: underline_r <= inf_{b_0,b_1 in R} int (y^2 - b_0 - b_1 y)^2 d nu_a(y) for each a in {0,1}.

flags.f2_scaffold_directive: FAITHFULNESS + SUBSTRATE DISCHARGE (persistent). @realizes tag-space; derive-not-assume note-proved facts; universal constants OUTERMOST; all domain hyps; thm:global-log-rate conclusion is ⨆ nu∈MBand. FRONTIER FULLY REMOVED: the closed-form-frontier cluster (oeq/class-frontier/frontier-duality/rho-envelope) is DELETED from the plan — do NOT scaffold ANY of it, do NOT reference dualRho/rhoEnvelope/threePointLaw/classFrontier/quartic/IsLUB anywhere. ONLY targets: thm:global-log-rate + thm:instance-local-minimax + their supporting lemmas. VAN-TREES DISCHARGE (I-1 built): NO hVT hypothesis on the theorem statements — supply Causalean.Stat.Limit.VanTreesInequality.van_trees_inequality in the proofs. SUBSTRATE REUSE (built): lem:local-neighborhood-cumulative-risk reuses Causalean.Stat.Limit.SequentialCumulativeRisk.sequential_cumulative_risk_regret (supply van_trees_inequality to its van-Trees hyp); armTangentStrength=L2ResidualQuadratic; armScoreCost=ConstrainedQuadraticScoreProgram.scoreCost_eq; linear_tilt_path KL=KlDensityTiltExpansion.klDiv_tilt_expansion. Do NOT re-flag needs-substrate.

proposed_from.topic: First matching minimax LOWER bound for Neyman regret in adaptive two-arm experiments, resolving the open problem of Dai et al. (arXiv:2411.14341 Logarithmic Neyman Regret): they prove the upper bound R_T <= O~(f(pi*) log T) for the Clipped-Second-Moment-Tracking design but prove no matching lower bound. Establish the minimax lower bound over bounded-outcome adaptive two-arm instances via a change-of-measure / epoched two-point (Le Cam) construction tying detection-time of the unknown second moments to accumulated quadratic Neyman loss V(pi_t)-V(pi*), pinning the SHARP minimax Neyman-regret rate (accumulated variance-inefficiency of the adaptive Horvitz-Thompson ATE estimator vs the oracle Neyman allocation pi*=S1/(S0+S1)); exact overlap dependence determined by the lower-bound proof, not fixed in advance. Distinct from simple-regret/best-arm minimax (arXiv:2309.08808, 2512.08513) which concern chosen-arm welfare, not estimator variance-suboptimality.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"closest_published_results":[{"bibkey":"neopane2024logarithmic","precise_gap":"Gives the superpopulation `O(log T)` upper bound for ClipSMT but no lower bound for the same cumulative Neyman-regret functional."},{"bibkey":"dai2023clipogd","precise_gap":"Defines Neyman regret and proves only an `O(sqrt(T))` upper bound in the design-based setting, without any converse."},{"bibkey":"kato2024simple","precise_gap":"Provides a minimax simple-regret frontier for best-arm identification under Neyman allocation, but the loss is final welfare error rather than cumulative variance inefficiency of an adaptive HT estimator."},{"bibkey":"ma2021minimaxope","precise_gap":"Provides minimax lower bounds for inverse-weighted estimation risk, but not for adaptive designs whose logging rule itself is chosen to learn `pi_nu_star`."}],"why_the_kernel_is_not_a_relabel":"The paper kernel targets `sum_t {V_nu(pi_t)-V_nu(pi_nu_star)}`, so the hard object is not choosing the better arm but learning the variance-minimizing allocation quickly enough to avoid accumulated quadratic variance loss. That requires mean-preserving second-moment perturbations, a local curvature calculation for `g_nu`, and a change-of-measure argument tied to adaptive propensities.","chosen_reason":"This angle is the sharpest surviving theorem-level object that matches the D-1.1 gap directly, has a credible proof route from adjacent lower-bound machinery, and cleanly supports a concrete open-ended residual question about the closed-form frontier."}
proposed_from.chosen_specialization: adaptive two-arm experiment; first matching minimax lower bound pinning the sharp minimax Neyman-regret rate
seed_list: Local-to-global converse for adaptive Neyman regret: prove an instance-local lower bound `mathfrak_R_T(Alg,nu) >= c kappa_nu log T` and a uniform `Omega(log T)` minimax corollary for bounded-outcome two-arm ATE experiments.
Closed-form lower-bound frontier: solve the extremal bounded-outcome program for `kappa_nu` and identify the exact overlap and second-moment factor multiplying `log T`.
Fixed-population converse: prove the first design-based `Omega(log T)` lower bound against the hindsight Neyman allocation in the finite-population Clip-OGD model.
Population-separation theorem: show the superpopulation and finite-population Neyman-regret frontiers are genuinely different, not just proof artifacts.
Boundary-overlap frontier: characterize how the lower-bound constant changes when `pi_nu_star` approaches `0` or `1`, rather than inheriting upper-bound clipping exponents.
Contextual multigroup converse: prove the first lower bound for multigroup Neyman regret with overlapping groups.
Regret-inference tradeoff: show that any design beating the logarithmic Neyman-regret frontier must pay a martingale-inference price for HT/AIPW estimation.

Loss-frontier divergence: formalize why simple-regret lower bounds and Neyman-regret lower bounds share a variance-learning bottleneck locally but differ globally because Neyman regret accumulates quadratic loss each round.
proposed_from.last_draft_handoff: {"status":"completed","message":"Revised core written to proto_core.json with primitive tangent-regular class predicates, corrected local-complexity well-posedness, the balanced-base-law sanity check, and anchored Neopane/Noarov/Zhao comparator positioning.","artifacts":["/<repo-root>/CausalSmith/doc/formalization/research/stat_neyman_regret_minimax/discovery/proto_core.json"],"literature_checklist":[{"author":"Ojash Neopane, Aaditya Ramdas, Aarti Singh","year":2024,"venue":"arXiv","bibkey":"neopane2024logarithmic","one_line":"Abstract and Section 1 state that ClipSMT improves the superpopulation Neyman-regret upper rate from O(sqrt(T)) to O(log T), which is the upper-bound benchmark the new converse is meant to match.","relevant_to":"thm:global-log-rate"},{"author":"Georgy Noarov, Riccardo Fogliato, Martin Bertran, Aaron Roth","year":2025,"venue":"arXiv","bibkey":"noarov2025stronger","one_line":"Theorem 3.2 and equation (1) give an anytime design-based O~(log T) Neyman-regret upper bound under stronger lower-bounded outcomes, sharpening the published rate benchmark without providing a minimax converse.","relevant_to":"thm:global-log-rate"},{"author":"Jinglong Zhao","year":2023,"venue":"arXiv","bibkey":"zhao2023adaptive","one_line":"The paper explicitly frames adaptive Neyman allocation as learning unknown arm standard deviations across stages, making it the closest positive comparator for the variance-learning bottleneck behind the local lower bound.","relevant_to":"thm:instance-local-minimax"},{"author":"Jessica Dai, Paula Gradu, Christopher Harshaw","year":2023,"venue":"NeurIPS / arXiv","bibkey":"dai2023clipogd","one_line":"This paper introduced Neyman regret for adaptive Horvitz-Thompson ATE estimation and proved the first O~(sqrt(T)) design-based upper bound, setting the regret functional the converse targets.","relevant_to":"thm:instance-local-minimax"},{"author":"Cheng Ma, Botao Zhu, Jiantao Jiao, Martin J. Wainwright","year":2021,"venue":"arXiv","bibkey":"ma2021minimaxope","one_line":"Its information-theoretic OPE lower bounds provide the closest inverse-weighting Le Cam template, but not mean-preserving second-moment perturbations for adaptive Neyman regret.","relevant_to":"lem:path-existence"},{"author":"Masahiro Kato","year":2025,"venue":"arXiv","bibkey":"kato2025treatmentchoice","one_line":"The treatment-choice simple-regret lower bounds provide the nearest adaptive change-of-measure converse template, but for welfare loss rather than cumulative variance inefficiency.","relevant_to":"thm:instance-local-minimax"}]}

seed_details.one_line: Local-to-global converse for adaptive Neyman regret: prove an instance-local lower bound `mathfrak_R_T(Alg,nu) >= c kappa_nu log T` and a uniform `Omega(log T)` minimax corollary for bounded-outcome two-arm ATE experiments.
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Prove the first matching minimax lower bound for cumulative Neyman regret in adaptive bounded-outcome two-arm ATE experiments.
seed_details.referee_objection: Immediate from simple-regret change-of-measure papers because both problems only need to learn arm standard deviations.
seed_details.rebuttal: Simple regret charges the terminal treatment choice, while Neyman regret charges `sum_t {V_nu(pi_t)-V_nu(pi_nu_star)}`, so the proof needs a new quadratic loss conversion from second-moment uncertainty to cumulative variance inefficiency.

seed_details.one_line: Closed-form lower-bound frontier: solve the extremal bounded-outcome program for `kappa_nu` and identify the exact overlap and second-moment factor multiplying `log T`.
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Determine the exact overlap and second-moment dependence in the logarithmic minimax Neyman-regret lower bound.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is only a constant-sharpening appendix once the `log T` lower bound is known.
seed_details.rebuttal: The extremal program decides which published overlap and clipping factors are information-theoretic and which are proof artifacts, so it is a named frontier object, not cosmetic polishing.

seed_details.one_line: Fixed-population converse: prove the first design-based `Omega(log T)` lower bound against the hindsight Neyman allocation in the finite-population Clip-OGD model.
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Separate the fixed-design, design-based minimax Neyman-regret rate from the superpopulation rate, and prove the matching lower bound in the fixed finite-population potential-outcomes model.
seed_details.referee_objection: Once Noarov et al. give near-logarithmic upper bounds, the converse is routine.
seed_details.rebuttal: No published paper proves a design-based lower bound for cumulative HT variance regret; the fixed-population proof must replace i.i.d. local alternatives by hidden potential-outcome tables and is materially different.

seed_details.one_line: Population-separation theorem: show the superpopulation and finite-population Neyman-regret frontiers are genuinely different, not just proof artifacts.
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Separate the fixed-design, design-based minimax Neyman-regret rate from the superpopulation rate.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a framing theorem without a new frontier object.
seed_details.rebuttal: It becomes real only if one exhibits distinct lower-bound complexities on the two models; absent that, it is not sharp enough for the headline.

seed_details.one_line: Boundary-overlap frontier: characterize how the lower-bound constant changes when `pi_nu_star` approaches `0` or `1`, rather than inheriting upper-bound clipping exponents.
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Determine the exact overlap and second-moment dependence in the logarithmic minimax Neyman-regret lower bound.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is subsumed by any successful solution of the full `kappa_nu` program.
seed_details.rebuttal: Correct; as a standalone angle it is too narrow, so it survives only as a slice of the closed-form frontier question.

seed_details.one_line: Contextual multigroup converse: prove the first lower bound for multigroup Neyman regret with overlapping groups.
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Extend sharp minimax lower bounds to contextual and multigroup Neyman regret while preserving valid inference.
seed_details.referee_objection: The multigroup target is too far from the two-arm noncontextual kernel to close in one step.
seed_details.rebuttal: The objection lands for a first version; the noncontextual lower bound is the necessary base case, while multigroup lower bounds are a follow-on consumer.

seed_details.one_line: Regret-inference tradeoff: show that any design beating the logarithmic Neyman-regret frontier must pay a martingale-inference price for HT/AIPW estimation.
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Extend sharp minimax lower bounds from Horvitz-Thompson Neyman regret to estimator-aware adaptive designs while preserving valid inference.
seed_details.referee_objection: This bundles two hard theorems and risks producing only a vague Pareto slogan.
seed_details.rebuttal: That risk is real; it is better treated as a downstream consumer once the pure regret converse is in place.

seed_details.one_line: Loss-frontier divergence: formalize why simple-regret lower bounds and Neyman-regret lower bounds share a variance-learning bottleneck locally but differ globally because Neyman regret accumulates quadratic loss each round.
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Prove the first matching minimax lower bound for cumulative Neyman regret in adaptive bounded-outcome two-arm ATE experiments.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is only a comparison lemma, not a standalone paper kernel.
seed_details.rebuttal: Correct; it is valuable comparator-clearing prose and perhaps a proposition inside the chosen angle, but not the headline theorem.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:arm-tangent-nondegeneracy / ass:tangent-band
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The logarithmic lower bound is conditional on the tangent-regular bounded-outcome regime excluding arm laws where second-moment perturbations are degenerate or nearly degenerate.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The headline is a sharp minimax Neyman-regret lower bound on the tangent-regular class M_tan and its compact subclass M(underline_m, overline_m, underline_r), not on all bounded-outcome laws.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: oeq:closed-form-frontier
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The constant-exact class frontier K and finite-support extremal law are explicitly left open rather than assumed.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The flagship claim is the Omega(log T) minimax lower-bound rate with kappa_nu as an information-program handle; closed-form constants are supplementary open content.

banked_reason: Certified core banked (gate-free, sorry-free, axiom-clean: kappa_nu closed form, neyman_gap_identity, recursion-to-Omega(log T) engine, derived band continuity, class/score construction); full Omega(log T) converse kept as CONDITIONAL extension on the broad LocalNeighborhoodRiskInputs gate (F2.5 flagged it as laundering the note's derived recursion/domination/sup), documented not certified.
